description_long: This object is a rectangular, black TP-Link network switch, featuring a matte finish and a sturdy, boxy construction. The front panel displays eight Ethernet ports arranged in a single row, each labeled numerically from 1 to 8, with subtle white text and port outlines. On the left side of the front face, the TP-Link logo and product description are printed in white and yellow, while the right side features a small power indicator. The top, sides, and rear of the device are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing its utilitarian design. The device is constructed primarily from hard plastic with internal metal components for structural support and heat dissipation. This type of device is commonly found in office buildings, server rooms, or utility closets within urban environments.
description: A black, rectangular TP-Link network switch with eight front-facing Ethernet ports and a matte finish. The front panel features labeled ports, a logo, and a power indicator.
description_view_0: Front view showing eight Ethernet ports, TP-Link branding, and a power indicator.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain, featureless black surface.
description_view_2: Rear view with a smooth, unmarked black panel and no visible ports or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, also plain and featureless.
attribute_port_arrangement: 8 Ethernet ports in a row, labeled 1-8
